requirements.summary: Implement Google Sign-In on the Login page with profile setup flow
acceptanceCriteria: Login page displays a functional 'Continue with Google' button
Clicking the button triggers the Google Identity Services sign-in flow
On successful Google authentication, the parsed JWT user data (name, email, picture) is persisted via the existing useGoogleAuth hook
If no profile exists for the authenticated user, the ProfileSetupModal opens pre-filled with Google account name
If a profile already exists, the user is redirected to the home page
Google sign-in state is reflected in the Layout/navbar (shows user avatar or name from Google auth)

file_operations.description: Add a 'Continue with Google' button that triggers the Google Sign-In flow via the useGoogleAuth hook. On successful authentication, check if a user profile exists using useGetCallerUserProfile. If no profile exists, open the ProfileSetupModal with pre-filled data from the Google JWT (displayName, avatarUrl). If a profile exists, navigate to the home page. Ensure the button is styled consistently with existing auth buttons and positioned prominently on the page.

Integrate Google authentication state from useGoogleAuth hook alongside existing Internet Identity state. When a Google session is active (useGoogleAuth returns a user object), display the Google profile picture as a circular avatar in the navbar. Ensure the avatar is clickable and navigates to the Profile page. Add a sign-out option in the user menu that clears the Google session via the useGoogleAuth clear method. Show the sign-in button only when neither Google nor Internet Identity sessions are active.

requirements.summary: Implement Google Sign-Up on the Signup page with automatic profile setup flow
acceptanceCriteria: Signup page displays a functional 'Join with Google' button
Clicking the button triggers the Google Identity Services sign-in flow
On successful Google authentication, the useGoogleAuth hook stores the user data
ProfileSetupModal is shown automatically with channel name pre-filled from Google display name
After profile setup is saved via useSaveCallerUserProfile, the user is navigated to the home page
Duplicate sign-in (existing profile) redirects user to home without reopening setup modal

file_operations.description: Add a 'Join with Google' button that triggers the Google Sign-In flow via the useGoogleAuth hook. On successful authentication, check if a user profile already exists using useGetCallerUserProfile. If no profile exists, open the ProfileSetupModal with pre-filled data from the Google JWT (displayName, avatarUrl). If a profile exists (duplicate sign-in), navigate directly to the home page. Ensure the button is styled consistently with existing signup options and positioned prominently below or alongside the email/password form.

requirements.summary: Update ProfileSetupModal to accept and display Google auth pre-fill data
acceptanceCriteria: ProfileSetupModal accepts optional googleDisplayName and googleAvatarUrl props
When googleDisplayName is provided, the channel name field is pre-filled with it
When googleAvatarUrl is provided, it is shown as the avatar preview in the modal
User can still override the pre-filled values before submitting
Modal submission saves profile correctly with any user-edited values

file_operations.description: Add optional props googleDisplayName (string) and googleAvatarUrl (string) to the component. When googleDisplayName is provided, pre-populate the channel name input field with this value using the useState initial state or useEffect. When googleAvatarUrl is provided, display it as the initial avatar preview image in the modal. Ensure users can still edit the pre-filled channel name and upload a different avatar if desired. The form submission logic should use the final (possibly user-modified) values when calling useSaveCallerUserProfile.

requirements.summary: Update Layout/navbar to display Google user profile with navigation and sign-out
acceptanceCriteria: When a Google session is active (useGoogleAuth returns a user), the navbar shows the Google profile picture as a circular avatar
Hovering or clicking the avatar shows the user's Google display name
Clicking the avatar navigates to the Profile page
A sign-out option is accessible from the user avatar menu for Google-authenticated users
When neither Google nor Internet Identity session is active, the sign-in button is visible

file_operations.description: This file operation is covered by REQ-1 (modify Layout.tsx to integrate Google auth state). Ensure the implementation includes: (1) displaying the Google profile picture from useGoogleAuth as a circular avatar when a Google session is active, (2) showing the Google display name on hover or in a dropdown menu, (3) making the avatar clickable to navigate to /profile, (4) adding a sign-out menu item that calls the useGoogleAuth clear method and clears cached data via queryClient.clear(), and (5) conditionally showing the sign-in button only when both Google and Internet Identity sessions are inactive.
